messages.content: You are a practical, friendly assistant for everyday how-to advice.
I want a realistic weekly plan to learn Spanish while working full-time. I can do about 30 minutes a day and maybe a bit more on weekends. What should I do each day?

Here’s a simple, sustainable plan that balances input (listening/reading), output (speaking/writing), and review. Aim for 30 minutes on weekdays and 45–60 minutes on one weekend day.

Core tools
- One app or course for structure (e.g., a beginner course or app), an SRS flashcard deck for vocabulary, a podcast with transcripts, and a voice recorder for speaking practice.

Weekly structure
- Mon: 15 min course lesson + 15 min SRS review
- Tue: 15 min listening (podcast episode segment with transcript) + 15 min read-aloud/shadowing
- Wed: 15 min course lesson + 15 min speaking drills (record yourself answering 5 prompts)
- Thu: 15 min SRS + 15 min writing (5–8 sentences about your day; get quick corrections if possible)
- Fri: 20 min listening review (same episode or a new short one) + 10 min pronunciation work (shadow tough lines)
- Sat or Sun (45–60 min):
  • 20 min course lesson consolidation
  • 15–20 min conversation: language exchange or speaking to yourself using a prompt list
  • 10–20 min SRS cleanup and adding new words from the week

Daily tactics
- Anchor time: Attach sessions to an existing routine (e.g., after breakfast, or commute for listening-only tasks).
- Micro-goals: Define exactly what “done” means each day (e.g., finish Lesson 8A, add 10 new cards, shadow 20 lines).
- Rotation: Revisit the same content 2–3 times in the week (listen → read with transcript → shadow) to deepen learning.

Speaking focus without a partner
- Use prompts: “Describe your morning,” “What did you do yesterday?” Record 60–90 seconds; re-record once focusing on clarity.
- Shadowing: Repeat after audio in short chunks, matching rhythm and intonation.

Vocabulary and review
- Cap new words at ~10/day. Always add example sentences, not just isolated words.
- Keep SRS sessions short; if the queue grows, pause adding new cards for 2–3 days.

Tracking and motivation
- Keep a simple habit tracker. If you miss a day, do a 10-minute “catch-up lite” the next day and resume the plan—don’t double up.

After 4–6 weeks, bump one weekday to a 45-minute session focused on conversation practice or intensifying listening, depending on your goals.
